enonce.texte:
Exercise 1: Environmental Protection Vocabulary

Read the following words and match them with their correct definitions. Write the letter of the correct definition next to each word.

questions.texte: Conservation
questions.reponse: B - The protection of natural resources and wildlife

questions.texte: Preservation
questions.reponse: D - Keeping something in its original state

questions.texte: Protection
questions.reponse: A - Keeping something safe from harm or damage

questions.texte: Recycle
questions.reponse: C - Converting waste into reusable material

questions.texte: Reduce waste
questions.reponse: E - Decreasing the amount of garbage produced

enonce.texte:
Exercise 2: Complete the Sentences

Complete the following sentences with the correct words related to environmental protection from the box.

conservation • preservation • recycle • reduce • protection • sustainable • eco-friendly

questions.texte: We should __________ plastic bottles instead of throwing them away.
questions.reponse: recycle

questions.texte: __________ of natural resources is essential for future generations.
questions.reponse: Conservation

questions.texte: __________ energy sources like solar and wind power are better for the environment.
questions.reponse: Sustainable

questions.texte: We must __________ our forests from deforestation.
questions.reponse: protect

questions.texte: Using __________ products helps reduce pollution.
questions.reponse: eco-friendly

enonce.texte:
Exercise 3: True or False

Read each statement about environmental protection and decide if it is True or False. Correct any false statements.

questions.texte: Planting trees helps reduce air pollution.
questions.reponse: True

questions.texte: Burning waste is a good way to protect the environment.
questions.reponse: False. Burning waste releases harmful gases and contributes to air pollution.

questions.texte: Recycling paper saves trees and reduces deforestation.
questions.reponse: True

questions.texte: Using plastic bags is better for the environment than using reusable bags.
questions.reponse: False. Plastic bags are not biodegradable and cause environmental damage.

questions.texte: Turning off lights when leaving a room helps conserve energy.
questions.reponse: True

enonce.texte:
Exercise 4: Analyse and Connect

Read the following environmental problems and describe two possible solutions for each using vocabulary related to environmental protection.

questions.texte: Problem: Deforestation in Cameroon
questions.reponse: Solution 1: Plant trees (reforestation). Solution 2: Enforce laws against illegal logging (protection/conservation).

questions.texte: Problem: Plastic pollution in rivers and oceans
questions.reponse: Solution 1: Reduce plastic use (reduce waste). Solution 2: Recycle plastic products (recycle).

questions.texte: Problem: Air pollution from car exhaust
questions.reponse: Solution 1: Use public transport or carpool (reduce emissions). Solution 2: Use electric or hybrid vehicles (sustainable energy).

enonce.texte:
Exercise 5: Write a Campaign Message

Imagine you are organizing a campaign to protect the environment in your community. Write a short message (80-100 words) to encourage people to take action. Use at least five vocabulary words related to environmental protection.

questions.texte: Write your campaign message here.
questions.reponse: Dear community members, we must act now to protect our environment. Conservation of our forests and water resources is essential for our survival. Please recycle your waste and reduce plastic use. Let's preserve our natural resources for future generations. Join us in planting trees this weekend! Together, we can make a difference. Remember: small actions lead to big changes.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 6: Problem-Solution Essay

Read the following problem and write a short essay (120-150 words) proposing solutions.

Problem: In your city, people are throwing garbage in the streets and rivers. This causes pollution, health problems, and harms wildlife. What can be done to solve this problem?

questions.texte: Write your essay here.
questions.reponse: The problem of waste disposal in our city is a serious environmental issue. People throw garbage in the streets and rivers, causing pollution and health problems. To solve this problem, the city authorities should install more public bins and collect waste regularly. Additionally, we must raise awareness about the importance of recycling and reducing waste. Community clean-up campaigns can also help keep our neighbourhoods clean. The government should enforce laws against littering and punish offenders. Finally, schools should teach students about environmental protection and conservation. If we all work together, we can create a cleaner and healthier environment for everyone.
